content:
Welcome to September! 

At this point, I guess we plan to see crew utility in SAGE around BreakPoint? 

Does anyone know if the external factors holding us up are getting fixed?

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
Yes, things are being fixed with the new DAS API. Obviously, I don’t know the exact timing, but we’re getting closer. I think it’s all about making changes to the already minted cNFTs (from episode 0 to episode 1) before giving them utility. There is a cap on the number of calls you can make on those APIs, so that’s why we need time.

I think they’re not saying anything because the update has already been delayed twice. I’m 100% sure they’re waiting to be completely ready to announce it in order to prevent a hypothetical third delay!

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I don't think it will have a big impact on "user growth", but I think it will have a massive impact on small/mid size player user happiness.  However I think it will be a key opportunity for new players coming into Galia to pursue from the top of the funnel.    Every SB + every resource will now become a new market.  A player can now become the "Food Prince of MRZ-25".  All he does is produce food at MRZ-18 and haul it to MRZ-25.  Those smaller niches are key.  It's a form of specialization.   It will save a lot of us a lot of fuel and time (from having to go to CSS).

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: Well, this is a subset of the marketplace v3 development we presently have prioritized going in to Q4. So I'm not implying we'd hold it back for more players. But naturally, this feature benefits from more players in the game, given greater liquidity at local markets. And I'd prefer to work towards player growth as we go into the bull market over quality of life improvements (as much as I hate to say it). Generally we want you all to continue having a great time (or as good a time as possible). But growth in players leads to a more dynamic economy for everyone.

author: DominicVainMod
content: Yep, understood. I'm sure you realize how sensitive we are to dates being publicly shared because of the constant misconception of our targets. Nevertheless, I did largely update the community on our projected roadmap going in to the end of this year on the last town hall. Did you catch that? Otherwise, we were actually anticipating crew cards earlier in the year. And the only intended functionality/utility beyond PFPs is to be added to Based (announcement on this pending). Ship configuration, crew configuration, etc. is all coming later. On the UE side, heavy emphasis on getting Fleet Command on feature parity with Based, and rolling out FPS modes. These are predominantly "top of funnel" user acquisition features with the intention of getting people interested in the game (F2P modes on Fleet Command). Then we work on conversion. Otherwise, the roadmap as it's outlined I believe has already been updated ( @DominicVain)? We dropped the R2.2 release, rolling those features and the new releases into "Preseason" and work towards the larger Season 0 relerase after that.

author: ROME | wikiwikiwik
content: GM @Chri.z , I listened to the economic forum and love the idea of trying out the RFR concept again in starbased and found the idea of rotating recipes interesting, Santi mentioned rotating recipes could hurt the "fun" aspect of planning things out if it constantly changes- but on the other hand, no changes could become less interesting over time. To keep things interesting and give opportunity for those who like to plan, could the recipes rotate like once a month (or whatever cadence makes sense for you to measure economic impact) and the next recipe would be selected via a simple DAO vote? Dan said the hardest part of DAOs are cultivating engagement. Currently, the best way to create DAO engagement is to tie it to Sage gameplay and it would be a low risk way to start tinkering with how PVP could affect gameplay. Trying to keep dev work as light as possible to accomplish this, a process could be you create 3 or 4 recipes for players to choose from. The overarching principle behind the design of the recipes is to create decisions for players to focus more on LP loop, RFR loop or ship redemption loop (or hybrid). The PVP holders would vote on which recipe they want the most for the next month. For example- RFR recipe A has resources required that might affect whether a player chooses to install Emags at starbases or they may decide to allocate those resources into the RFR loop. Then recipe B may affect electronics which could do interesting things to the mamba ex loop and/or LP installs at starbases. Recipe C might effect field stabilizers or a combination of components and so on. You mentioned its not hard to actually go into the game and update the crafting recipes, so once the voting period ends, the team goes in and updates the RFR recipe for the next time frame. If the recipe is a flop for whatever reason, you might present a different set of recipes for the next vote.

author: ROME | wikiwikiwik
content: To help with DAO engagement, could try putting a notice at the top of the Labs screen that says "Click here to go vote for the Council's next RFR request" that takes them to the DAO pip screen where they choose either recipe A, B, C or D. Keeping in mind, we are always watching our competitors on Eve Eye...a voting mechanism could provide a way for us to try and use PVP to influence competitor behavior when we know certain starbases are on deck for upgrading.Make people choose whether they want to risk installing field stabilizers at the shiny new starbase....or just go for the RFR instead.

author: questionoak
content:
Screenshot #1  - Used Tensor to try to find a crew and got no  results when I search for this crew. I navigated to this page under Star Atlas on Tensor. -- Note the left bar
Screenshot #2 - Used Tensor to find the same crew and found it when I navigate from the top right wallet/inventory. -- Note the left bar and see I actually have 3 collections in my wallet.
Screenshot #3 - This is a screenshot of the crew in my WALLET  (Note the highlighted tab and the top of the popup) after I filtered for Common. I use this page to copy and paste into Tensor because that is the only way that they can find my crew. 

My point is that the ONLY way I can find crew and sell them is going from my spreadsheet or Based/Wallet copying and pasting for Tensor to find crew. I can't imagine this bug is just mine. I spent the time to find a workaround on their site and I did it for a few high dollar crew, but I am not going to for hundreds of common and uncommon. I can't be the only person with a big bag facing this bug so if it is widespread - it is lost revenue for Tensor and SA... It also might be distorting the market, because I have only been a buyer the past couple weeks.

@SW4GN3R, thx I just saw your response - i.e. that you have given Tensor an exclusivity window. That is all I need to know which is why I thought I was asking a simple question in that room, i.e. are we going to work with other exchanges (ME in particular)?  I will wait and maybe they will either fix their bug or ME comes online.

author: questionoak
content: For sure. We're going to list on ME in the future. As you noted, however, they already pull down the listings from Tensor (and vice versa with Tensor). I'd still recommend chatting directly with those exchanges to discuss various requests though. Also, as @Deleted User mentioned, nobody has deployed a cNFT at this scale before. Which is indeed creating mainnet issues with the DAS API at scale.

reply.preview: For sure. We're going to list on ME in the future. As you noted, however, they already pull down the listings from Tensor (and vice versa with Tensor). I'd still recommend chatting directly with those exchanges to discuss various requests though. Also, as @Deleted User mentioned, nobody has deployed a cNFT at this scale before. Which is indeed creating mainnet issues with the DAS API at scale.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I am well aware of the issues with what you are doing with your scale and it is why I have only soft launched one of my companies. We ended up building our own marketplace for FractionMotors. With just six cars at 100,000 shares each, we had all sorts of problems on a testnet. I am fine keeping it under wraps as we build out more. We cache everything we can to make the experience as easy as possible in a browser for web3 guys who know how to use a wallet. We completely hide the wallet experience when a person uses our apps on their phone (i.e. when they QR scan a car).  I think you will find that Tensor and ME will only get you so far until you build a SA crew marketplace and you can cache everything except for the state and transaction itself.

author: questionoak
content: I dont think tools sent to Naabathon need to be "private", I dont think to infringe anything by sharing this before the Naabathon starts/ends.  This is pretty much a mockup for what I want to do but I'm not here to present the software or apologize for the current state of it    Go to the My Crew section on the left, then put all of your wallets (included the SagePlayerProfile if you have already sent your crew into Sage) and click Search button. When the research is done, click on Save CSV and you will have a CSV file with all your crew, pretty similar to the sheet you have shared but you can do it all the times you want by yourself, if you buy new crew members.   A csv file is not user friendly  but you can import it into Excel or a Google Sheet to "beautyfy" it! Maybe you dont need it anymore, sorry for being late!    https: //crewforge.netlify.app/

content: Hey New Kids on the Block.... @🅰🅲🅴🅺🅸🅽🅶 and @[SAI] Torinder ⪛๏⫺ You are showing you know nothing about how this journey even started. LOL at the guys that bought all the C11s and Treearrows in the GAO. They funded you being here today, so show some respect... Hopefully a few are still around. Yes... $99.900. author: Mandrake [SKR]
content:
Hey guys y’all losing sight of what we are doing and what we are here for. It’s extremely rough going but we are moving forward. Resources are limited! Time is limited! Finances are limited! There are many people pushing for products to be launched all the time. It’s difficult to juggle all these. Try to understand 

Y’all cannot let these smaller issues get to you. That is a very tough way to live. 

Looking at the grand scheme of things it was still an awesome event! 

Crypto is still a developing space. We all need to be tolerant of bugs and issues. You have to expected it and also “Accept” it.

We got more than 800k crews that are extremely complex with extreme number of options. I don’t think any project out there even comes close.

author: Lorddo [ALRT]
content: The script handles two main use cases,   import-all-crew Allows a user to provide a direct connection (bypassing the UI for the sake of saving time) and transfer CREW to any player profile they want. This automates the entire process of moving crew in the most efficient manner. The benefit of doing this increases the more crew you have to move.   The benefits here also extend to giving users the option to choose and RPC, specify their own pritority fees etc. All the improvements we made along with this script were added directly to the SAGE UI, to give everyone access to these improvements.   transfer-profile Allows you to move a player profile authority from one wallet to another. While this has many use cases, the one specific to this application is so that players who have thousands of crew in a cold wallet, and want a way to migrate  without pressing their ledgers tens of thousands of times, can do so with this script by transferring the profile to a new cold wallet, converting the old cold wallet to a hot wallet, and then execute the import-all-crew script without having to approve each txn manually.

reply.preview: The script handles two main use cases, import-all-crew Allows a user to provide a direct connection (bypassing the UI for the sake of saving time) and transfer CREW to any player profile they want. This automates the entire process of moving crew in the most efficient manner. The benefit of doing this increases the more crew you have to move. The benefits here also extend to giving users the option to choose and RPC, specify their own pritority fees etc. All the improvements we made along with this script were added directly to the SAGE UI, to give everyone access to these improvements. transfer-profile Allows you to move a player profile authority from one wallet to another. While this has many use cases, the one specific to this application is so that players who have thousands of crew in a cold wallet, and want a way to migrate without pressing their ledgers tens of thousands of times, can do so with this script by transferring the profile to a new cold wallet, converting the old cold wallet to a hot wallet, and then execute the import-all-crew script without having to approve each txn manually.

author: questionoak
content:
Just want to check before I create a ticket in case it is a known issue. I did close my browser and started with a clean instance. I just tried to move 17 crew as a test before moving hundreds to just see if they work.  Six were transferred to Sage.

I am using a Solflare and a Ledger and for those 17, I ended up approving in Solflare 12 times (each time three transactions except for the last one which had two transactions). I approved on my Ledger 35 times. I watched as the crew slowly went down as the Solflare popups were being created. Sometimes the crew dropped and sometimes they didn't - I suspected maybe caching was delaying the actual number. Also, sometimes I had to check that I "trust the transactions" and other times I didn't (maybe 50/50). When I approved the two transaction request in Solflare, I thought I was done. Strangely I then started getting more Solflare transactions. I waited to see if the crew number would keep going down, but in fact it started going going up???  It now looks like only six of the crew moved? I keep closing Solflare popups (at least twenty times). Do I close the browser and start again? wait for a fix? or approve the popup Solflare approvals and keep spending Sol.
Something is not right here and the software should definitely catch the problem and not keep asking me to spend Sol.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I believe your entire vote goes to one candidate. If that candidate doesn't qualify for the top 12 (in this round), of final 5 (next round), then it goes 100% to the next eligible candidate that has a chance of winning. That way your vote isn't "wasted"

author: ROME | Zamsor ⪛⦿⫺ [ROME]
content: If there are not enough selections your vote goes with the other voters on your most viable candidate. That’s a precise way of saying we guess that you would vote like other people voted. That’s why it’s important to rank as many as you can to make sure your opinions are followed

reply.preview: If there are not enough selections your vote goes with the other voters on your most viable candidate. That’s a precise way of saying we guess that you would vote like other people voted. That’s why it’s important to rank as many as you can to make sure your opinions are followed

author: PG I BULK
content:
Star Atlas is a highly ambitious and innovative blockchain-based gaming project that combines elements of space exploration, strategy, and NFT-based assets. Given the context of Breakpoint in Singapore, an event known for showcasing the latest in blockchain technology and innovation, it's plausible that Star Atlas could present a range of exciting updates and new features. Here are some educated guesses on what they might present:

Gameplay Enhancements:New Game Features: They might introduce new gameplay mechanics, missions, or events that enhance the player experience.,Expanded Universe: Announcements about new sectors, planets, or star systems being added to the game.,,
Technological Advancements:Blockchain Integrations: Updates on how they're leveraging blockchain technology, including improvements to their use of the Solana blockchain for faster and more secure transactions.,Cross-Chain Compatibility: Potential integration with other blockchains to expand their ecosystem and user base.,,
NFT and Asset Updates:New NFT Collections: Introduction of new ships, items, or characters that players can purchase, trade, and use within the game.,Enhanced NFT Utility: Ways in which existing NFTs can be used in more interactive or rewarding ways within the game.,,
Partnerships and Collaborations:Strategic Partnerships: Announcements of new partnerships with other blockchain projects, gaming companies, or technology providers to enhance the game.,Collaborations with Artists and Creators: Collaborations with renowned artists or creators to bring unique content and assets to the game.,,
Community and Ecosystem Development:DAO and Governance: Updates on the development of their Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) and how players can participate in the decision-making process.,Community Events and Initiatives: Plans for upcoming community events, competitions, or initiatives to engage and grow their player base.,,
Technical Roadmap and Future Plans:Development Roadmap: Detailed roadmap outlining the future development milestones and features they plan to implement.,Vision for the Future: Insights into the long-term vision of Star Atlas and how they see the game and its ecosystem evolving.,,
By presenting these updates at Breakpoint, Star Atlas can not only showcase their progress and innovations but also generate excitement and engagement within the blockchain and gaming communities.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: I will not enter into polemics, last time was enough for me, just my opinion:  In this iteration of the game, the whale will always receive the best conditions, no matter what mechanics you introduce, the very idea that bodhi or someone else wants extra preferences is not true, yours, his, and even my volumes are more than enough to extract maximum benefit from any meta,SDU was great for small players, but keep in mind mechanics changed, you cant scan with xxs anymore, increased demand will help those who survived last 4 months and still have small/mid fleets, but cost of entrance will be higher. What I really like – people who play manually will have advantage; question is will benefits from moving fleets to “green” zones be enough to encourage small player to do it with there own hands. If you have 3k fleet for example (~3opods) with some maxhogs for transporting fuel and SDU how much would you get playing manually 8h a day? Lets round it to 50k SDU per day, with current SDU prices you will have 6250atlas minus fuel cost, lets say SDU price will double, 12.5k, i`m lazy to count it, ok 10k atlas per day playing 8h a day, 20$, 5month ROI, not bad on paper, but its still 20$ playing manually, playing on bot will give you 1year ROI, not bad with “whales” investments, not attractive for newcomer with small/mid pockets. Just my thought on SDU topic,Also let me remind you, last time we had “holy war” team picked your position, I know that was easy pick, coz you was saying “all changes are great and they are aimed at supporting small players”, but suddenly we lost almost all of them. Atlas price is universal argument with which I cannot argue, but I still believe things could go better with out some changes we had after SB release. My personal income in USDC went much higher after all patches we had, this is not “helping small players sign” and I earn same atlas with same price,

content: He says smaller players are happy, cause all the ones that aren’t happy already left lol.  Another issue I see in regards to #2. author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: @Lorddo The team made it clear that bases are not intended to cap before combat. SDU supply is already raising as we get closer to the SDU wall, with nearly 3k MambaX in circulation. Slight SDU sink outside bases wont hurt nor will it help, but a large enough sink can halt upgrades past t2 during the 4 month GT event. The least reconcilable consequence of this is reducing the demand for crew. The deposit side accounts for an average of %39 of crew demand across all components. If deposits halts due to the impending sdu wall, crew will effectively have ~%39 less utility.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree

author: Lorddo [ALRT]
content: Using non mining ships for mining doesn’t have to do with the SDU wall, not sure what you mean.   The SDU wall is fundamentally a scarcity of sdu relative to the other upgrade mats in regards to base upgrades. This can lead to a situation where all bases in a faction are full of everything other than SDU.   SDU production has maintained highs of 6m per day globally for three months. As long as there are T1 bases that only require 3m SDU, bases can continue to upgrade. Once there are no longer any T1 bases in a faction, the requirements jump to 20-28m SDU per base. This mean that on average it will take a faction 10 days minimum to fill a base with the needed SDU. 20m/(6m/3) and 2 weeks for T5. The necessary non sdu components are produced and deposited far quicker than 10-14 days. It only takes 50k crew to outpace sdu accept for t5 taking 70k crew to outpace SDU due to PA.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: Bodhi is just fishing for SDU strategy now.  I know it was very enlightening for me.  Thank you. .  Always a fine line when trying to argue a game strategy is good/bad, X or Y, when it ultimately comes down to player strategy and use.  It sounds like Factions should be optimizing for SDUs.  If they continue down this route of 100% frameworks they are actually putting themselves in a situation where they are creating the bottleneck themselves. And why should that be rewarded by making a gameplay fix? It's from the players very own actions.  Instead, it sounds like  proper planning of upgrades and SDU scanning would mitigate a lot of this issue.  Great discussion.

author: [SH]b0b [STAR]
content:
I agree atlas discount looks a bit unfair to people who bought at higher prices, but I like it as long that helps attracting funding to the project. Player base is stable right now, we are all in the same boat. I think team should chase the spread, if its below 10-15% from time to time - we are fine, big sales not good ofc. 
What i really don`t like and wished to change - components drop situation. Originally i thought we are having components drop same date we have crew drop, but we are still far from ship constructor module, we do not know how much components will cost, how hard it will be to craft them, it effects ship market alot. No one can be sure if price for hull is good or bad right now, i personally have plans for few big ships to purchase, but im really affraid to be surpriced with components cost and availability.
It will also be an unpleasant surprise for new players. Yes we have disclaimer on market, but lets be fair, people can miss or misundestand that "sold separatly" tag.
I would suggest to make another snapshot for components before module release, that will be better for players and project financing, ship market almost dead right now ;/

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content:
We're still going to get the component bundles out soon, but honestly, it's not going to provide much transparency into future pricing until those bundles are split into isolated components, which will come with Ship Configuration. That's a ways out. Also, none of the ships require any of those components or modules right now. They all perform the same as they did prior to the snapshot. So if your ship had "upgraded" components previously, they still function the same. Crew is the big differentiator now.

Also, every ship still comes with t0 non-removable components. They won't have weapons outfitted on them though. Technically buying a ship now performs as though it was pre-snapshot from the component standpoint. But the "hull" is largely functional from a base operating standpoint with the exception of weapon systems. 

So, suffice to say, whether or not those bundles are airdropped yet, it isn't really going to matter. Because the market can't enter price discovery until those items have utility in game. When ship config comes online, we'll also provide crafting recipes for these components/weapons/modules.

edit: jumping through messages and this was kind of scrambled together. But the key point is that the bundles don't really matter until ship config anyway

reply.preview: Message could not be loaded

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: I wouldn't necessarily categorize these shortcomings as "mistakes." We realize we need greater depth in game systems. And we have plans for the full game, which you can read in the SAGE Game Manual. It's simply that we only have bandwidth to produce so many features before a release if we want to do so in a relatively short iterative fashion. Would you rather wait 2 years for a more polished product, or 3 months for something that is semi-baked? My assumption, and indications from users, is that it's the latter. You get something to play while we keep building.

author: [SAI] Torinder ⪛๏⫺ [SKR]
content: I wouldn't necessarily categorize these shortcomings as "mistakes." We realize we need greater depth in game systems. And we have plans for the full game, which you can read in the SAGE Game Manual. It's simply that we only have bandwidth to produce so many features before a release if we want to do so in a relatively short iterative fashion. Would you rather wait 2 years for a more polished product, or 3 months for something that is semi-baked? My assumption, and indications from users, is that it's the latter. You get something to play while we keep building.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Thanks for the clarification, close release of bundles will really improve the situation on the secondary market, it's a pity this won't affect direct sales from the team. I completely agree with you, but I look at things from the player and investor's point of view. All ships of the large category and above, except for miners and transport, are purchases for the future. The efficiency of 1 commander in Sage is equal to ~30 Mamba EX, which can be crafted or bought at much cheaper prices due to high supply. "Honest" prices for such ships have naturally formed on the market, players know the current price of C11, but without a bundle, pricing is completely broken. During the summit, it was repeatedly said that the main advantage of web3 gaming compared to traditional games is the ability to both buy and later sell your game assets. The period when the real price of an asset is unknown is a bad period in the history of the project. Tbh its not a big deal in SA, we have strong community, everyone i now and interact here - are diamong hand believers, but that doesnt change santiment.  Speaking of "mistakes" i don`t want to develop this topic, coz It's easy to say in hindsight, but keeping/adjusting RFR and adding Based would be easy solution that could work well. From player perspective i feel we changed the rules of the game too radically and than it took too long to react on weak spots of current gameplay. But again these are small shortcomings compared to the huge world that you guys are making for us.

But again these are small shortcomings compared to the huge world that you guys are making for us.

author: [DEEP] Digital Fists
content: Still need to respond to questions in FR, but we have a huge content schedule through next year all the way up to next Breakpoint. I’d also emphasize that ultimately we need to build something insulated from market cycles. A lot of gamers won’t necessarily care what Bitcoin price is, just whether or not they have a good gaming experience. Which is where the focus lies, and it’s coupled with a strong, stable internal economy. That’s the path to long term viable success. But yes, we’re certainly looking to capitalize revenues during the bull market and set us up to get this thing to the finish line.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: I totally agree SA shouldn't rely on market cycles long-term. In fact it shouldn't rely on new investors at all. Creating additional revenue streams through commerce, advertising, live service gaming, etc is the best way to ensure long-term success imo. Every other anything "to-earn" project that relies on new investors is just a ponzi doomed to fail.  But more short-term, I think runway is a very common worry for a lot of people in the community. People like to be reassured that ATMTA won't run out of money on the way to creating the self-sustaining vision.
